description: Add npm registry search to the opentabs CLI so users can discover available plugins. The `opentabs plugin search <query>` command queries the npm registry for packages with the 'opentabs-plugin' keyword, displays results in a formatted table with official/community labels, and handles edge cases like no results, network errors, and missing query arguments.

userStories.title: Add opentabs plugin search command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search the npm registry for opentabs plugins so that I can discover and install plugins without leaving the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs plugin search <query> hits the npm registry search API: GET https://registry.npmjs.org/-/v1/search?text=keywords:opentabs-plugin+<query>&size=20
Results are displayed in a formatted table with columns: name, description, version, author
@opentabs-dev/* packages are labeled [official] in the output
All other packages are labeled [community] in the output
Each result shows install instructions: npm install -g <package-name>
The search subcommand is registered under opentabs plugin search
opentabs plugin --help shows the search subcommand
userStories.notes: Edit platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts. Add a handlePluginSearch function and register a new 'search' subcommand under the plugin command. The handler takes an optional query argument. Use fetch() to call the npm registry search API — the endpoint is https://registry.npmjs.org/-/v1/search?text=keywords:opentabs-plugin+<query>&size=20. The response shape is { objects: Array<{ package: { name, description, version, publisher: { username }, date } }> }. Format the output using picocolors (already imported). For the label, check if name starts with '@opentabs-dev/' — if so, show pc.blue('[official]'), otherwise pc.dim('[community]'). Truncate descriptions to ~60 chars to keep table width manageable. After the table, show a blank line and then: 'Install a plugin: npm install -g <package-name>'. Use the existing commander pattern from the create/list/add/remove subcommands as a template.

userStories.title: Handle edge cases and improve UX
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear feedback when search has no results, the network is down, or I run the command without a query so that the experience is polished and helpful.
acceptanceCriteria: No results: displays 'No plugins found for '<query>'. Try a different search term.'
Network error (fetch throws): displays 'Could not reach npm registry. Check your internet connection.'
opentabs plugin search with no query argument lists all opentabs plugins (omits the query term from the URL, effectively searching by keyword only)
HTTP 429 response: displays 'npm registry rate limit reached. Try again in a moment.'
Non-200 HTTP responses (other than 429): displays 'npm registry returned an error (HTTP <status>). Try again later.'
Invalid JSON response: displays the network error message rather than crashing
userStories.notes: Edit the handlePluginSearch function in platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts. Make the query argument optional in the commander definition (.argument('[query]', ...) instead of .argument('<query>', ...)). When query is undefined, omit it from the URL — the search becomes just keywords:opentabs-plugin which returns all matching packages sorted by popularity. Wrap the entire fetch + JSON parse in a try/catch. Inside the try: check response.status — if 429, show rate limit message; if not ok, show generic HTTP error. If response.ok, parse JSON and check objects.length === 0 for the no-results case. In the catch: show the network error message. Use pc.yellow for warnings, pc.red for errors.

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md to document the plugin search command
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want CLAUDE.md to document the opentabs plugin search command so that the project instructions stay accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md CLI commands section mentions opentabs plugin search
The description accurately reflects what the command does
userStories.notes: Edit CLAUDE.md. In the CLI section where commands are listed (the 'Commands' section under 'CLI'), add a line for 'opentabs plugin search <query>' or update the plugin command description to mention the search subcommand. Keep it brief — this file should remain plugin-agnostic per the existing guidelines, so just document the command existence, not specific plugins it might find. Also update the registerPluginCommand description comment in plugin.ts if it still says 'list, add, remove, create' to include 'search'.
